pr.title: Provider Profiles Schema Migration and Architecture Fixes

comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The type hint for `clear_env_keys` is `Mapped[Optional[dict[str, Any]]]`, but it is used as a list of strings in other parts of the code, such as the Pydantic models (`ProviderProfileCreate`, `ProviderProfileResponse`) and the data seeding logic. For consistency and to avoid potential errors, this should be updated to `Mapped[Optional[list[str]]]`.

```suggestion
    clear_env_keys: Mapped[Optional[list[str]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
```

## Code Review

This pull request migrates the 'Auth Profile' system to a comprehensive 'Provider Profile' architecture to support multi-provider and multi-mode credentials for managed agent runtimes. Key changes include renaming database tables and SQLAlchemy models, updating Pydantic schemas for provider-aware selection, and implementing a strict environment layering model with launch-time secret resolution. The feedback identifies a type hint inconsistency in the database model for `clear_env_keys`, which should be updated to a list of strings for consistency with the rest of the implementation.

## Unused import Import of 'sa' is not used. --- To fix the problem, remove the unused import of sqlalchemy from this migration file. In general, unused imports should be deleted to keep the code clean and avoid unnecessary dependencies.

Concretely, in api_service/migrations/versions/4b53a4790314_rename_tables_to_provider_profiles.py, delete the line import sqlalchemy as sa (line 11 in the snippet). No other lines need to be modified, and no new imports, methods, or definitions are required. The remaining from alembic import op import is sufficient for the operations used in upgrade and downgrade.

## Unused import Import of 'op' is not used. --- To fix the problem, remove the unused import of op from Alembic, leaving only the imports that are actually used. This eliminates the warning without changing functionality, since op is not referenced anywhere.

Concretely, in api_service/migrations/versions/61c25ebc7201_merge_heads_before_provider_profiles.py, delete the line from alembic import op (line 10 in the snippet). No other changes are required: no new methods, imports, or definitions are needed, and existing behavior (empty upgrade/downgrade) is preserved.

## Unused import Import of 'sa' is not used. --- To fix the problem, remove the unused sqlalchemy import from this migration file, keeping only imports that are actually referenced. In general, unused-import issues are solved by either deleting the import or adding code that legitimately uses it; here, adding dummy usage would be pointless and would not change behavior, while deleting is safe because no code depends on sa.

Concretely, in api_service/migrations/versions/61c25ebc7201_merge_heads_before_provider_profiles.py, delete the line import sqlalchemy as sa (line 11 in the snippet). No other changes are required: the typing and alembic.op imports are still used by the type hints and potential migration logic, while sa is not. No new methods or imports are needed.

## Unused global variable The global variable 'branch_labels' is not used. --- In general, to fix an “unused global variable” warning, either remove the unused global or ensure it is genuinely used. For Alembic migration identifiers, if optional globals are not needed (e.g., they are None and not relied upon by tooling), the cleanest fix is to delete the unused declarations.

Here, branch_labels and depends_on are both declared and set to None but not used elsewhere in the file. The safest way to eliminate the CodeQL warning without changing runtime behavior is to remove these two lines entirely and keep only the required identifiers revision and down_revision. No new imports or additional code are needed.

Concretely, in api_service/migrations/versions/053758f254f3_update_provider_profiles_schema.py, remove lines 18–19:

branch_labels: Union[str, Sequence[str], None] = None
depends_on: Union[str, Sequence[str], None] = None

and leave the rest of the file unchanged.

## Unused global variable The global variable 'depends_on' is not used. --- In general, the way to fix an unused global variable is either to remove it if it is not needed, or to use it meaningfully if it exists for a real purpose. For Alembic migrations, the depends_on variable is only required when you want to declare that this revision depends on another; otherwise, omitting it is equivalent to defining it as None.

The best fix here, without changing existing functionality, is to remove the depends_on assignment entirely. Alembic will behave the same, since None is the implicit default, and no other code in the snippet references depends_on. Only this line (line 19) in api_service/migrations/versions/053758f254f3_update_provider_profiles_schema.py needs to be removed. No additional imports or definitions are required.

Provider profile `env_template` validation currently calls `_contains_sensitive_key`, which rejects common *environment variable names* like `*_API_KEY` / `*_AUTH_TOKEN`. Provider profiles need to set these keys (with placeholders/refs), so this will make the create/update endpoints unusable for most real profiles. Adjust validation to allow sensitive-looking keys and instead validate that values are non-secret placeholders/refs (or use an allowlist via `allowed_sensitive_keys`).

Schema types for provider-profile fields don’t match the documented/usage expectations: `tags` should be a list of strings (not a dict), `clear_env_keys` should be a list of strings, and `file_templates` is defined as a dict but is described/used like a list. These mismatches will lead to FastAPI response_model validation errors and/or incorrect selection behavior in the manager when tags are used.

Model field types for JSON columns look incorrect for the new provider profile contract: `tags` and `clear_env_keys` are typed as dicts but should be JSON arrays (list[str]), and `file_templates` is typed as a dict but should be a JSON array (list[...]). With the current typing, any list stored in these columns can break downstream serialization/validation (e.g., API response models, manager tag filtering).
```suggestion
    tags: Mapped[Optional[list[str]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
    priority: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, nullable=False, default=100, server_default=text("100"))

    volume_ref: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)
    volume_mount_path: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(512), nullable=True)
    
    secret_refs: Mapped[Optional[dict[str, Any]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
    clear_env_keys: Mapped[Optional[list[str]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
    env_template: Mapped[Optional[dict[str, Any]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
    file_templates: Mapped[Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
```

Manager sync payload defaults/types for provider-profile JSON fields appear inconsistent: `tags` defaults to `{}` and `file_templates` defaults to `{}`, but these fields are expected to be list-like (tags list, file_templates list). This can cause manager-side tag filtering to behave incorrectly (e.g., treating a dict as an iterable of keys) and can confuse consumers of the sync payload.
```suggestion
        "tags": row.tags or [],
        "priority": row.priority,
        "secret_refs": row.secret_refs or {},
        "clear_env_keys": row.clear_env_keys or [],
        "env_template": row.env_template or {},
        "file_templates": row.file_templates or [],
```

Runtime mapping for loading profiles looks wrong for Gemini: the submit runtime values elsewhere in this file use `"gemini"`, but this mapper checks for `"gemini_cli"`, which will return "" and hide/break profile loading for Gemini runs. This should likely key off `"gemini"` (similar to `claude`/`codex`).
```suggestion
    if (m === "gemini") {
```

## Pull request overview This PR completes the migration from legacy “Auth Profiles” to “Provider Profiles” across the API service, Temporal workflows, runtime launcher behavior, and the task dashboard, aligning code and docs toward provider-aware selection, env layering, and secret indirection. **Changes:** - Introduces provider-aware profile selection (provider/tags/priority/materialization mode) and wires `profile_selector` through `AgentExecutionRequest` → `AgentRun` → manager. - Updates runtime launching to layer `os.environ`, support `clear_env_keys`, and resolve `secret_refs` placeholders at launch time. - Renames/updates DB schema + API endpoints and refreshes unit tests and documentation/spec artifacts accordingly. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 54 out of 54 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2) **moonmind/workflows/temporal/artifacts.py:2114** * `auth_profile_list` is used as the DB snapshot for AgentRun→manager sync and for ManagedAgentAdapter profile shaping. Mapping `auth_mode` to `credential_source` (e.g. `oauth_volume`) will cause ManagedAgentAdapter to treat OAuth profiles as API-key mode (it checks `auth_mode == "oauth"`). Also, the returned profile dict omits new manager-critical fields (`provider_id`, `tags`, `priority`, `runtime_materialization_mode` is present but the rest aren’t), so provider/tag selection and priority sorting won’t work until an out-of-band sync occurs. Consider: (1) keep `auth_mode` as legacy `oauth|api_key` for adapter compatibility, and (2) include `provider_id`/`tags`/`priority`/`runtime_materialization_mode` in the snapshot payload the manager consumes. ``` { "profile_id": row.profile_id, "runtime_id": row.runtime_id, "auth_mode": row.credential_source.value, "credential_source": row.credential_source.value, "runtime_materialization_mode": row.runtime_materialization_mode.value, "volume_ref": row.volume_ref, "volume_mount_path": row.volume_mount_path, "account_label": row.account_label, "api_key_ref": None, "runtime_env_overrides": row.env_template or {}, "api_key_env_var": None, "max_parallel_runs": row.max_parallel_runs, "cooldown_after_429_seconds": row.cooldown_after_429_seconds, "rate_limit_policy": row.rate_limit_policy.value, "max_lease_duration_seconds": row.max_lease_duration_seconds, "enabled": row.enabled, } ``` **api_service/main.py:595** * `ensure_provider_profile_managers_started()` starts the manager workflows but never sends an initial `sync_profiles` signal. Because AgentRun’s `_sync_manager_profiles()` relies on `auth_profile.list` (which currently returns legacy-shaped rows), a freshly started manager may lack provider-aware fields like `provider_id/tags/priority` until the first API mutation occurs. Consider syncing current provider profiles immediately after starting each manager (or ensure `auth_profile.list` includes the full provider profile fields). ``` logger.info("Ensuring AuthProfileManager workflows are started...") try: async with get_async_session_context() as session: stmt = select(ManagedAgentProviderProfile.runtime_id).distinct() result = await session.execute(stmt) runtime_ids = result.scalars().all() if not runtime_ids: logger.info("No managed agent provider profiles found. Skipping AuthProfileManager startup.") return temporal_adapter = TemporalClientAdapter() temporal_client = await temporal_adapter.get_client() for runtime_id in runtime_ids: workflow_id = f"auth-profile-manager:{runtime_id}" try: await temporal_client.start_workflow( WORKFLOW_NAME, AuthProfileManagerInput(runtime_id=runtime_id), id=workflow_id, task_queue="mm.workflow", ) logger.info(f"Started AuthProfileManager for runtime: {runtime_id}") except WorkflowAlreadyStartedError: logger.debug(f"AuthProfileManager already running for runtime: {runtime_id}") except Exception as e: logger.error(f"Failed to start AuthProfileManager for {runtime_id}: {e}", exc_info=True) ```

## Unused import Import of 'Sequence' is not used. --- To fix the problem, remove the unused Sequence import while preserving the used Union import. This keeps the file functionally identical while satisfying the linter.

Concretely, in api_service/migrations/versions/053758f254f3_update_provider_profiles_schema.py, adjust the import on line 8 from from typing import Sequence, Union to from typing import Union. No other code changes are required, and no new imports or definitions are needed.

## Unused import Import of 'Sequence' is not used. --- To fix the problem, remove the unused Sequence from the typing import while keeping Union, which is used for the down_revision type annotation. This preserves existing type behavior and functionality.

Concretely, in api_service/migrations/versions/4b53a4790314_rename_tables_to_provider_profiles.py, edit the import on line 8 so that it imports only Union. No other changes are needed, and no additional methods or definitions are required.

## Unused import Import of 'ProfileSelector' is not used. --- To fix the problem, remove the unused import so that the module no longer brings in ProfileSelector. This eliminates the dead code, resolves the CodeQL warning, and does not affect existing functionality since ProfileSelector is never referenced.

Concretely, in api_service/api/routers/auth_profiles.py, delete the line:

from moonmind.schemas.agent_runtime_models import ProfileSelector

No additional methods, imports, or definitions are needed; we only remove this single unused import line.

## Unused global variable The global variable 'branch_labels' is not used. ---

In general, the way to fix an "unused global variable" in a migration script is to either remove the variable if it is not needed or, if it must exist for framework/tooling reasons, ensure it is actually used in a meaningful way. For Alembic migrations, only the identifiers that Alembic inspects (revision, down_revision, and sometimes depends_on / branch_labels when relevant) need to be present; optional ones can be omitted when unused.

For this specific file, the best fix that preserves existing functionality is to delete the branch_labels global, since it is set to None and not used anywhere. Alembic will still correctly identify the migration using revision and down_revision, and depends_on remains available if needed in the future. No imports or additional definitions are necessary; we simply remove line 13.

Concretely:

  • Edit api_service/migrations/versions/61c25ebc7201_merge_heads_before_provider_profiles.py.
  • Remove the line defining branch_labels.
  • Leave the rest of the file unchanged.

## Unused import Import of 'Sequence' is not used. --- To fix an unused import, remove the specific name that is not used while preserving any that are. In this file, only Union is actually referenced, so we should stop importing Sequence and keep the Union import unchanged.

Concretely, in api_service/migrations/versions/61c25ebc7201_merge_heads_before_provider_profiles.py, update the import on line 8 from from typing import Sequence, Union to from typing import Union. No other lines need modification, because there is no reference to Sequence anywhere else in the snippet. This preserves the existing type annotation for down_revision and removes the unused import that CodeQL flagged.
